rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al VS. HAYLEE YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver, Motion For Modification Of Injunction, And Imposition Of Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver, Motion For Modification Of Injunction, And Imposition Of Attorneys Fees Continued on the court's own motion to March 6, 2013 to give Defendants the opportunity to file their opposition and comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Although the Register of Actions does not reflect that an opposition was filed, Plaintiff filed a substantive reply brief to the opposition. The Court does not have a courtesy copy of any opposition, either. Defendants shall file their opposition with the filing clerk and deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers previously served on Plaintiff to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 22, 2013 at 4:00 pm, or the motion will be granted. The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SELMA OZER VS. FRANZ ZENZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Motion Toc Ompel;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF SELMA OZER'S MOTION TO COMPEL. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Parties to appear with proposed orders. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THIRD STREET LOFTS ASSOCIATION VS. THIRD STREEET LOFTS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Strike Supplemental Expert Witness Designation Of Defendant Third Street Lofts Llc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2013, Line 2, Plaintiff's Motion To Strike Supplemental Expert Witness Designation Of Defendant Third Street Lofts LLC Denied. Defendant Third Streets Lofts LLC has shown good cause to designate Mr. Fitzpatrick as an expert witness. Plaintiff has not shown prejudice. Plaintiff has known of the Axis Construction cost estimate, about which he will opine, was distributed to all counsel in June 2012. Defendant shall make Mr. Fitzpatrick available for deposition as soon as possible. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MELINDA BARANY et al VS. MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Written Discovedry And For Sanctions [Ccp 2030.290, 2033.280, 2031.300]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 11, DEFENDANT MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel PlAINtIFf'S Responses To Written Discovery And For Sanctions [Ccp 2030.290, 2033.280, 2031.300]. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant motion to compel responses to discovery. Plaintiff Barany shall provide responses without objections by March 8, 2013. Defendant's request to deem RFAs admitted denied for failure to include in the notice of motion. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $2,026.50. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SUNTECH AMERICA, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. ACRO ENERGY TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Admotion To Set Aside Dismissal And For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF SUNTECH AMERICA, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And For Entry Of Judgment Hearing required. The court is inclined to grant the motion, in part, as no opposition is filed, provided that Plaintiffs bring proofs of service of the ex parte application, order shortening time and motion on the settling Defendants. The parties settled for $ 1,200,000.00. (Ng Decl., Exhibit 1, § 3(a).) The parties agreed that upon default, judgment could be entered for the principal amount of $ 1,812,854.45 less any payments, plus interest of 21%. (Id. at § 5, Exhibit C.) However, the settlement provides for installment payments totaling less than the full amount in the complaint, but requiring the full amount due in the event of a default This is an unenforceable penalty. (Greentree Fin'l Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 495, 499-501.) The court is inclined to grant judgment of $1,200,000.00 less any payments. Plaintiffs should be prepared to calculate interest on this lesser amount, and to explain the basis for their calculation at the hearing. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY DEWITT VS. CRAZY PROTOCOL COMMUNICATIONS, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Ntc Re-Calendering Of Motion To Transfer And Consolidate For Hearing In Dept 302
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2013, Line 16, PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY DEWITT'S Re-Ntc Re-Calendering Of Motion To Transfer And Consolidate For Hearing In Dept 302 Denied. Plaintiff has failed to show that transfer and coordination is warranted or appropriate under Code of Civil Procedure sections 403 and 404.1. Common questions of law or fact do not sufficiently predominate over the two actions. Plaintiff has not shown that he would be disadvantaged by inconsistent rulings in these different cases. Coordination does not benefit the convenience of the parties, witnesses, and counsel sufficient to transfer a case from Alameda County to this court.. Plaintiff has not shown that coordinating the cases would serve the efficient utilization of judicial facilities and resources. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Brando Empire Sf And East West Bank To Produce Responsive Documents And Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 6, PLAINTIFF GOTHAM BAY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION'S MOTION To Compel Brando Empire Sf And East West Bank To Produce Responsive Documents And ReqUEST For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Parties to appear with proposed orders. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JANE ROE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 22, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, DEUTSCHE BANK AG, NICOLE DESANTIS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part with leave to amend, and the motion to strike is denied. The demurrer on the grounds that Plaintiff's use of a pseudonym is improper is overruled. Defendants have failed to show that use of a pseudonym is not appropriate under the circumstances of this case. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for quantum meruit is overruled. The quantum meruit cause of action is not barred by the contract cause of action or the argument that damages are determinable under the contract. Plaintiff is permitted to plead in the alternative. The demurrer to the eighth and ninth causes of action on statute of limitations grounds is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled application of the discovery rule at 64 and 74 of the Complaint. (See Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 806..) The demurrer to the eighth and ninth causes of action on particularity grounds is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead how the representations were made and by whom, including their authority to speak on behalf of the defendant corporation. (See Tarmann v. State Farm Mutual Auto. Ins., (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157.) The demurrer to the tenth cause of action is overruled and the motion to strike is denied on all grounds. The cause of action is sufficiently pled and it cannot be determined from the face of the complaint that the alleged defamatory statements are non-actionable opinion or barred by the common interest privilege found at Civil Code section 47(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
